pullRequest.title: DOM-54603 Fix Repoman not seeing new branches in remotes + basic repoman unit tests

nodes.body: This diff looks weird, I just moved the private constants below from the abstract class to the static object.
This is fixing the original issue, return all branches found in the git fetch rather than filtering by the branches repoman knows about.
Do you know why it used to only look at branches currently known by repoman? It doesn't make sense to me

No, this is why I was so reluctant to change this logic and was thinking of adding a separate method, but then I realized the `staleBranches` are only used in one place, so editing this would only affect that place which is what we intended to change. Maybe this was used differently in the past and then usages changed/evolved but this method didn't. Also cucu tests around this used to pass because they were all using the same branches that repoman knew about, but when we started having a unique branch per test, then this issue came to light.
This is to be able to mock the RepositoryProvider when testing the RepoManagerActor

Same here, the `config` was statically resolved before, now it can be mocked.
`repositoryProviderResolver` is the one I mentioned above, now we can mock what this resolver returns and do whatever we want in unit tests.
Thanks for cleaning this up to avoid going into RepositoryStrategy

You probably will need to add a default value for this in this application conf in charts: https://github.com/cerebrotech/charts/blob/master/domino/repoman/files/application.conf. I would check to see what happens in an fcmd deployment if you don't provide a default value. If you get some failures around the config value not being there, definitely add to the charts file. It's probably a good idea to add to the charts file anyway, so that all configuration values are transparent to engineers who work with charts.

Yes, thanks for pointing this out! It makes it fail with the EXPIRED_DEAD_LETTER error which would have been hard to debug if you hadn't commented this.
I'll add it to charts, but I think the APP should have a default anyways, I don't know how is that config file propagated to on prem customers, but in any case, if I add a new config that makes the APP break if not defined, is like adding a backwards incompatibility. So I added some fallback logic in case this is not present in the config file, it will default to true.

Would it be useful to a field engineer to see a Jira ticket key in the logs? is this useful information for debugging?

Can we just reference logger?  it should be available

It's easier for us to search in new relic for these logs by the jira ticket key and even build alerts on it. If someone else sees this and is curious to open the Jira ticket, they would see we are thinking about removing this code path, so they may even help us identify that this is being used somewhere. I didn't think of that case before, but maybe it would be more useful to make this a WARN log and say something like "This is being deprecated" so if someone that is not us sees this, will definitely try to reach out.

After some manual tests, I see this code is called for GitHub with PAT for example. Since there is no local clone, the `initiateClone` method does nothing when stale branches are detected. But it's used to invalidate the `fileMetaDataCache` when a branch repoman knows about changed in the remote.
When new branches are added to the remote in this case, there is yet another cache for this that has 60 seconds TTL, so the new branch is picked up when the entry for the list of branches is invalidated `domino.repoman.service.provider.GitHubProvider#fullBranchesQueryCache`.
I will remove these logs, since now we know this is being executed.
logger should just be available

This PR introduces drilling of objects through several layers of classes. I assume that this was to make the code more testable, which is great. This can become messy over time if we keep adding objects in this way. We might want to consider adding Guice or some other dependency injection framework at some point
Yes, I agree, should we create a Jira for that? I'll mention it in stand up, I don't know if we have some tech debt epic that we actively track, to avoid it getting lost in backlog.

here is the develop tech debt epic: https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-49138?jql=text%20~%20%22develop%20tech%20debt*%22

It seems more like a graveyard for tech debt tickets. tbh, repoman is pretty stable regarding the classes that exist inside of it. we aren't really adding much. maybe we should just keep guice in mind, but not file a ticket. it might not be super necessary
Why shouldn't this setting be here?

The GitProvider class currently has logic to interact with local clones, but it doesn't know when a local clone needs to be invalidated/replaced/refreshed, that is managed by RepoManagerActor. So this config should be there. But the problem is that the `staleBranches` method is also implemented for GitHub and GitLab APIs, when not using local clones. But repoman only uses this method in the context of local clones. So it's unclear why we have those implementations for GitHub/GitLab APIs (https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-55245).

For now the safest/easiest place to put this config was here, without affecting anything else. Ideally the `staleBranches` method should not take the `branches` parameter (which here is just a "filter by this branches"). So you call this and this gives you all branches new/modified in the remote. Then repoman based on the setting can decide if it uses all of them or filters them by the `branches` it knows about. This approach cannot be implemented unless we remove the code for GitHub/GitLab APIs that relies on this `branches` parameter, which makes no sense to me.
Why did you change this to be a class?

To be able to mock it where it was used. It's like all methods defined were all static before, and with this change they are instance methods now. So they can be mocked easily by passing a mock instance of this to the objects that use it.
got it
do you mean this?: "return new branches in the remote when configured to refresh local clone if new branches exist"
yes, thanks!
